concerns.text: Task T020 (detect_threshold.py) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T019 (stratify_accuracy.py) for binned accuracy data. A task consuming an artifact from another task cannot be parallel-safe with that producer. The [P] tag is incorrect and creates a false parallel execution path.

Task T022 (generate plots) is marked [P] but depends on T013 (annotate_graph.py) for continuous plot data and T019 for binned data. It cannot run in parallel with its producers. The [P] tag is invalid.

Task T025 (sensitivity.py) is marked [P] but the description explicitly states 'This task must run sequentially after T020 completes' and depends on T020 logic. The [P] tag contradicts the explicit dependency and ordering requirement.

Task T034 (fit_gam.py) depends on T013 (annotate_graph.py) for input data but is not listed in the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section under Phase 6 or US2 dependencies. While the task description mentions the dependency, the explicit ordering section fails to capture this critical data-flow dependency, risking T034 running before T013 completes.

Task T010 (Unit test for graph_utils) is marked [P] but depends on T006 (graph_utils.py). While tests often run after implementation, marking a test as parallel-safe with its implementation task is ambiguous. If T006 and T010 are meant to run in the same parallel batch, T010 will fail. If T010 waits for T006, it is not parallel with T006. The [P] tag should be removed or the dependency clarified.
